plugins.name: skill-audit-and-merge
plugins.description: Systematic audit and consolidation of skills marketplace plugins. Use when: (1) Marketplace has grown with overlapping plugins, (2) Need to identify merge candidates, (3) Improving plugin discoverability via tags, (4) Reorganizing categories for better structure.

plugins.name: skills-agent-field
plugins.description: Pattern for specifying which agent type should execute a skill. Use when: (1) creating specialized skills for specific agents, (2) routing Mojo skills to syntax validators, (3) directing GitHub skills to implementation engineers.

plugins.name: skills-registry-commands
plugins.description: Complete skills registry system with 4 components: (1) /advise command - search skills before starting work, (2) /retrospective command - capture session learnings as new skills, (3) documentation-patterns skill - how to write effective skill docs, (4) validation-workflow skill - CI/CD for plugin validation. Use when setting up team knowledge capture or implementing the Sionic AI skills pattern.

plugins.name: split-figures-per-tier
plugins.description: Pattern for splitting large faceted figures into per-tier subfigures to avoid Altair's 5,000-row dataset limit. Handles multi-facet cases and test pattern updates.

plugins.name: stale-script-cleanup
plugins.description: Remove stale one-time fix/migration scripts from a scripts/ directory safely. Use when: (1) scripts/ directory has accumulated one-time fix or migration scripts no longer needed, (2) cleaning up development artifacts before a release, (3) reducing cognitive load for new contributors by removing irrelevant scripts.

plugins.name: technical-debt-tracker
plugins.description: Track and resolve FIXME/TODO comments by creating GitHub issues and updating stale references. Use when: (1) Auditing codebase for technical debt, (2) Creating structured issues for each debt category, (3) Updating stale issue references in comments.

plugins.name: template-migration
plugins.description: Skill: template-migration. Use when working with template migration.

plugins.name: tier-progress-indicators
plugins.description: Add real-time progress tracking for parallel tier execution to prevent 'appears hung' issues. Use when parallel workers make it unclear how many tasks are complete vs still running.

plugins.name: tone-matched-documentation
plugins.description: Fill placeholder sections in documents while matching the author's existing writing style and tone

plugins.name: verify-already-implemented-issues
plugins.description: Verify issue implementation status before starting work by checking git log, branch state, and open PRs. Use when: (1) assigned to implement a GitHub issue in a pre-created worktree branch, (2) unsure if work is already done, (3) starting issue implementation and want to avoid duplicate work.

plugins.name: verify-issue-before-work
plugins.description: Verify GitHub issue state and existing implementation before starting work to avoid duplicate effort

plugins.name: worktree-cleanup
plugins.description: Remove merged or stale git worktrees. Use after PRs are merged, work is done, or when worktrees are no longer needed.

plugins.name: worktree-create
plugins.description: Create isolated git worktrees for parallel development. Use when working on multiple issues simultaneously.

plugins.name: worktree-prompt-already-done
plugins.description: Detect and confirm when a worktree issue prompt's implementation is already complete in git history. Use when: (1) invoked with a .claude-prompt-<N>.md file in a worktree branch, (2) git log shows the task commit as HEAD, (3) a PR already exists for the branch.

plugins.name: worktree-switch
plugins.description: Switch between git worktrees for parallel development. Use when working on multiple issues simultaneously.

plugins.name: worktree-sync
plugins.description: Sync git worktrees with remote and main branch changes. Use to keep long-running feature branches up-to-date.

plugins.name: spec-driven-experimentation
plugins.description: TECHSPEC.md pattern for structured ML experiments. Use when: (1) Planning parameter sweeps, (2) Defining success criteria before training, (3) Budget-constrained experiment design, (4) Hypothesis-driven ablation studies. Source: Sionic AI blog.

plugins.name: train-model
plugins.description: Execute model training with optimization algorithms. Use when running training loops on datasets.

plugins.name: stale-doc-count-audit
